response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web engineer with breadth across TypeScript, Node.js, and D, but the profile fundamentally mismatches this role. There is no evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency (a hard requirement), no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, no networking-security background (MACsec, 802.1X, captive portal), and only brief, dated C/C++ exposure (National Instruments, 2014–2015) rather than sustained C-for-UNIX development. Low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby shows systems aptitude, but not embedded LAN-switch firmware.
strengths: Solid low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers) at Dunnhumby
Broad long-tenured software career with strong testing/craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD)
Some C/C++ and Linux experience, plus embedded/hardware exposure (robotic arms, audio systems)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — a hard requirement for the role
No Linux kernel, networking (L2/L3), or security-technology experience (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF)
C experience is minimal and dated; recent career is web/full-stack, not embedded firmware

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or Linux kernel experience
General systems programming and testing discipline
